posts.title: LaTex 极其好用的数学公式输入方法
posts.text: LaTex原因前段时间，我产生了要输入数学公式的需求。经过收索后，最终我选择了LaTex。优点（我所见的）: 语法被markdown原生支持,且作为最为广泛被科学界（可能吧）使用的文章格式化工具，其同名的语法也被大多数的编辑器支持。 使用我总结了一个常用语法的思维导图，提取码:yxfh markdown使用需用：$ … $ or $$ … $$ 声明内容为sytax。 main thoughts:looks like a mark language normal text: subfix: eg: f_2 ; exam: $ f_2 $ supfix: eg: f^2 ; exam: $ f^2 $ sytax mark: algebra: algebra::blank : eg: f_1 \quad f_2 ; exam: $ f_1 \quad f_2 $ algebra::not equal: eg: \neq ;exam: $ \neq $ algebra::fraction : eg:f_2 = \frac{2}{5} ; exam: $ f_2 = \frac{2}{5} $ algebra::continuous fraction : eg: \begin{equation}x = 1 + \cfrac{2}{4 + \cfrac{2}{5 + x_1}}\end{equation} ; exam: $x = 1 + \cfrac{2}{4 + \cfrac{2}{5 + x_1}} $ algebra::square root : eg: x = \sqrt[3]{x_1^2 + x_2^2 + x_3^2} ; exam: $ x = \sqrt[3]{x_1^2 + x_2^2 + x_3^2} $ algebra::plus and minus : eg: \pm \mp ; exam: $ \pm \quad \mp $ algebra::trigonometric funcs : eg: \cos \sin \tan \cot ; exam: $ \cos \quad \sin \quad \tan \quad \cot $ algebra::limit : eg: \lim ; exam: $ \lim $ algebra::integral : eg: \int ; exam: $ \int $ algebra::sum : eg: \sum ; exam: $ \sum $ algebra::arrow : eg: \rightarrow or \to \leftarrow or \gets ; exam: $ \rightarrow \quad \to \quad \leftarrow \quad \gets $ algebra::dots : eg: \cdots \vdots \ddots ; exam: $ \cdots \quad \vdots \quad \ddots $ algebra::color : eg: {\color{red}xyz} ; exam: $ {\color{red}xyz} $ linear algebra: determiant::body : eg: \begin{array}{ccc} a_{1,1} & a_{1,2} & a_{1,3} \ a_{2,1} & a_{2,2} & a_{2,3} \ a_{3,1} & a_{3,2} & a_{3,3} \end{array} ; exam: $ \begin{array}{ccc} a_{1,1} & a_{1,2} & a_{1,3} \ a_{2,1} & a_{2,2} & a_{2,3} \ a_{3,1} & a_{3,2} & a_{3,3} \end{array} $ determiant::brakets : eg: \left | \right | ; exam: $ \left | xyz \right | $ determiant : eg:(\left | \begin{array}{ccc} a_{1,1} & a_{1,2} & a_{1,3} \ a_{2,1} & a_{2,2} & a_{2,3} \ a_{3,1} & a_{3,2} & a_{3,3} \end{array} \right |) ; exam: $ \left | \begin{array}{ccc} a_{1,1} & a_{1,2} & a_{1,3} \ a_{2,1} & a_{2,2} & a_{2,3} \ a_{3,1} & a_{3,2} & a_{3,3} \end{array} \right | $ matrix: eg:\begin{bmatrix} x & y \ z & v \ \end{bmatrix} ; exam: $ \begin{bmatrix}x & y \z & v \\end{bmatrix} $ small tips:要想输入特殊字符如：{ ，} ，[ ,]….可以使用许多编程语言中的转义符： *\*.

posts.title: c++ 魔幻写法
posts.text: 1for(int i = 0; i < scale ; elem[i++] = init_v); elem[i++] = init_v; ==== elem[i] = init_v; i++; 1elem[capacity = 2(to - from)] 赋值表达式返回值为左值,方便链式赋值 1234while (from < to){ elem[size++] = to_copy[from++];} 理由同1 1elem = new T[capacity <<= 1]; 利用位移操作符将int左移一位，按十进制的理解就是在数后面加零，效果就是数增大了一倍，而且根本不用考虑溢出。喵啊 `` c++while ((from < to–) && (e != _elem[hi]));```与1类似

posts.title: Stack vs Heap
posts.text: Stack VS Heap储存位置均在RAM 线程与heap和stack的关系在多线程的应用中，每一个线程都拥有一个属于自己的stack。但是所有这些线程都共享一个heap。因此，必须存在一些协调机制使得这些线程不会同时访问或操作heap。 一个对象object可以存在stack而不是heap上吗？可以，如果你在一个函数内create一个object而没有用new，那么这个object就存在stack上。如果这个函数运行完，那么stack上的object对应的内存也会被remove掉。如果希望在函数中create一个存在heap上的object，那么就要用new来实现。注意，如果用new开辟了一片空间，那么也必须要认为的delete掉这篇空间，否则将导致memory leak-占据已经无用的空间。 stack和heap上的memory生存时间stack上的空间当函数运行完自动释放，而heap上的空间只能由程序员delete才释放。 stack和heap可以grow in size吗？stack的大小是固定的，所以如果分配给stack的空间过大，就会stack overflow，一般出现在无限递归的情况下。而heap的当前空间不够用时，os就会再分配给heap一些空间。这一点是heap和stack的显著区别之一。 stack和heap是如何实现的？depends on语言,编译器,以及runtime。具体细节千差万别，但是总体上都是完成了相同的功能。 那个更快？为什么? 分配效率?stack更快，因为stack的空间分配只需要移动指针。 栈是机器系统提供的数据结构，计算机会在底层对栈提供支持：分配专门的寄存器存放栈的地址，压栈出栈都有专门的指令执行，这就决定了栈的 效率比较高。堆则是C/C++函数库提供的，它的机制是很复杂的，例如为了分配一块内存，库函数会按照一定的算法（具体的算法可以参考数据结构/操作系 统）在堆内存中搜索可用的足够大小的空间，如果没有足够大小的空间（可能是由于内存碎片太多），就有可能调用系统功能去增加程序数据段的内存空间，这样就 有机会分到足够大小的内存，然后进行返回。显然，堆的效率比栈要低得多。从这里我们可以看到，堆和栈相比，由于大量 new/delete的使用，容易造成大量的内存碎片；由于没有专门的系统支持，效率很低；由于可能引发 用户态和核心态的切换，内存的申请，代价变得更加昂贵。所以栈在程序中是应用最广泛的，就算是函数的调用也利用栈去完成，函数调用过程中的参数，返回地 址，EBP和局部变量都采用栈的方式存放。所以，我们推荐大家尽量用栈，而不是用堆。虽然栈有如此众多的好处，但是由于和堆相比不是那么灵活，有时候分配大量的内存空间，还是用堆好一些。 static用来控制变量的存储方式和可见性函数内部定义的变量，在程序执行到它的定义处时，编译器为它在栈上分配空间，函数在栈上分配的空间在此函数执行结束时会释放掉，这样就产生了一个问 题: 如果想将函数中此变量的值保存至下一次调用时，如何实现？ 最容易想到的方法是定义一个全局的变量，但定义为一个全局变量有许多缺点，最明显的缺点是破坏了此变量的访问范围（使得在此函数中定义的变量，不仅仅受此 函数控制）。需要一个数据对象为整个类而非某个对象服务,同时又力求不破坏类的封装性,即要求此成员隐藏在类的内部，对外不可见。静态数据成员要在程序一开始运行时就必须存在。因为函数在程序运行中被调用，所以静态数据成员不能在任何函数内分配空间和初始化。这样，它的空间分配有三个可能的地方，一是作为类的外部接口的头文件，那里有类声明；二是类定义的内部实现，那里有类的成员函数定义；三是应用程序的main（）函数前的全局数据声明和定义处。静态数据成员要实际地分配空间，故不能在类的声明中定义（只能声明数据成员）。类声明只声明一个类的“尺寸和规格”，并不进行实际的内存分配，所以在类声明中写成定义是错误的。它也不能在头文件中类声明的外部定义，因为那会造成在多个使用该类的源文件中，对其重复定义。static被引入以告知编译器，将变量存储在程序的静态存储区而非栈上空间，静态数据成员按定义出现的先后顺序依次初始化，注意静态成员嵌套时，要保证所嵌套的成员已经初始化了。消除时的顺序是初始化的反顺序。 static的优势可以节省内存，因为它是所有对象所公有的，因此，对多个对象来说，静态数据成员只存储一处，供所有对象共用。静态数据成员的值对每个对象都是一样，但它的值是可以更新的。只要对静态数据成员的值更新一次，保证所有对象存取更新后的相同的值，这样可以提高时间效率。 stack和heap可能会出现什么问题？sack空间不够用会stackoverflow，导致程序崩溃。而heap可能会出现fragamentation（碎片化），空间利用效率低下。 我应该用哪个？当明确知道你要使用的空间时，如果不大的话就用stack，如果较大或者无法暂时确定，就用heap。 C++对内存区的五种划分内存分配方式（五种） 全局/静态存储区域：用来存储全局变量，静态变量。程序编译时内存已分配好，并存在于程序整个运行期间，程序结束后由系统统一释放 全局变量和静态变量被分配到同一块内存中。 C 语言中，全局变量又分为初始化的和未初始化的。初始化的全局变量和静态变量在一块区域，未初始化的全局变量与静态变量在相邻的另一块区域。同时未被初始化的对象存储区可以通过 void* 来访问和操纵，程序结束后由系统自行释放。在 C++ 里面没有区分，他们共同占用同一块内存区。 栈：存放函数的参数值，局部变量，函数执行结束时会被自动释放。栈内存分配运算内置于处理器的指令集中，效率高，但是容量有限。 堆（动态内存分配）：通过new和malloc由低到高分配，由delete或free手动释放或者程序结束自动释放。动态内存的生存期人为决定，使用灵活。缺点是容易分配/释放不当容易造成内存泄漏，频繁分配/释放会产生大量内存碎片。 若程序员不释放，程序结束时可能由OS（操作系统）回收。 字符/文字常量区： 存放常量字符串，程序结束时由系统释放程序代码区： 存放函数体的二进制代码。 程序代码区： 存放函数体的二进制代码。 转自csdn

posts.title: 《津巴多普通心理学》-1，生物心理学与神经科学
posts.text: 虽然不怎么严谨，但是对于相信没有自由意志的人来说，这个结论也不必说。 人脑使用它巨大的神经回路网络调节身体的各种机能，控制我们的行为，产生我们的情感和欲望。并且加工人生中的各种体验。除此之外，人脑具有能够产生情绪，动机和洞察的贿赂，这是一般第难熬所不具备的。也许你觉得这些令人炫目的能力似乎远非我们头脑中的那些神经组织就能实现，但是是是不容争辩！当脑细胞因为疾病，药物或事故而受到损伤时，人类的心理过程是建立在生理基础之上的事实就显露无疑。因此，我们不得不承认人类的感官和知觉，学习和记忆，激情和痛苦甚至疯狂都有生理基础。 人脑结构成型和变化的影响因素 进化和基因（先天） 环境（后天） to be contined tips:人脑的结构也可以看作是基因型的表现型 心理过程的遗传学解释 。。。。 tips：一般一个心理现象（障碍）在少数情况下次啊是有一种基因决定的 身体内部通信 神经系统 内分泌系统 神经系统基本单位:神经元分类（功能和位置）： 感觉神经元（传入） 运动神经元（传出） 中间神经元 Agonists and Antagonistswhy side effects?brains contains many bundles of neurons which are called neural pathways and interconnect its components.Moreover,each pathway employs certain neuraltransmitters.Unfortunatly,there exsits some pathways which effect different functions using the certain agonists as the neuraltransmitters. Core conceptthe brain is composed of many specializex modules tha work togehter to create mind and behavior. left hemisphere and right hemisphere left: Regulation of positiveemotions Control of muscles usedin speech Control of sequence ofmovements Spontaneous speakingand writing Memory for words andnumbers Understanding speechand writingright: Regulation ofnegative emotions Responses to simplecommands Memory for shapesand music Interpreting spatialrelationships andvisual images Recognition of faces chapter summary**core concept 2.1:**Evolution has fundamentally shaped psychological processes because it favors genetic variations that produce adaptive behavior.**core concept 2.2:**The brain coordinates the body’s two communications systems, the nervous system and the endocrine system, which use similar chemical messengers to communicate with targets throughout the body.**core concept2.3:**The brain is composed of many specialized modules that work together to create mind and behavior.

posts.title: string类个人的（不完全）实现-c++
posts.text: 还有些功能没实现，比如>>操作符重载，find方法，还有insert方法有一些玄学问题。 吐槽： 为什么不能指定在heap中已分配的内存后再借着分配一段内存，每次只要要拓展字符串大小就要重新分配加复制，这可能要怪操作系统或者是我自己技术不行 每个由string类产生的新string只能在heap上分配内存，而且不调用析构函数的话，就只是类在stack中的成员变量被删除，但是heap中的字符串一直在那里，而且之前那个string其实一直在那。要解决这个问题其实可以在新创造一个string时把原来的删掉，但因为无法确定旧的string是否还有用，那就只能把选择权交给使用者了 Windows，fuck you！ 类名：string成员变量(private)： char * begin; int length;成员函数： constructor: string() string(const string &) string(const char *) //c语言的stirng string(const int,const char) deconstructor: ~string() method: int getLength() bool isEmpty() const char * c_str() friend std::ostream& operator<< (ostream&, const string&) friend bool operator== (const string&,const string&) friend bool operator!= (const string&,const string&) bool operator< (string&) bool operator<= (string&) bool operator> (string&) bool operator>= (string&) char& operator[] (int) bool isSame(const string&,int) string& substr(int,int) string& append(char) string& insert(int,const string&) string& erase(int,int) string.h: #include <cstddef>#include <iostream>class string{private: char* begin; size_t length;public: //constructor string():begin(nullptr),length(0){}; string(const string & obj):length(obj.length){ begin = new char[length+1]; for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) { begin[i] = obj.begin[i]; } begin[length] = '\0'; }; string(const char * obj){ int i = 0; for (;obj[i] != '\0';i++){ } length = i; begin = new char[length+1]; for (int i = 0; i < length; i++){ *(begin+i) = *(obj + i); } }; string(const size_t leng,const char ch):length(leng){ begin = new char[length+1]; for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) { begin[i] = ch; }; begin[length] = '\0'; }; //deconstructor ~string(){ delete[] begin; std::cout << "deconstructed successfully" << std::endl; }; int getlength(){ return length; }; bool isEmpty(){ return !length; }; const char* c_str(){ return begin; }; friend std::ostream& operator<< (std::ostream& ostr,string& str){ ostr << str.c_str(); return ostr; }; //friend std::istream& operator>> (std::istream& istr,string& str){} friend bool operator== (string& str1,string& str2){ if(str1.begin == str2.begin){ return true; }else if(str1.length != str2.length){ return false; }else{ return str1.isSame(str2,str1.length); } }; friend bool operator!= (string& str1,string& str2){ if(str1.begin == str2.begin){ return false; }else if(str1.length != str2.length){ return true; }else{ return !str1.isSame(str2,str1.length); } }; bool operator< (string& str2){ return length < str2.length; }; bool operator<= (string& str2){ return length <= str2.length; }; bool operator> (string& str2){ return length > str2.length; }; bool operator>= (string& str2){ return length >= str2.length; }; char& operator[] (int index){ return begin[index]; }; bool isSame(const string& str2,int length){ for(int i = 0;i < length;i++){ if(begin[i] != str2.begin[i]){ return false; } } return true; }; string& substr(int posi,int len){ char * temp = new char[len+1]; if(posi+len >= length){ return *(new string()); }else{ for (int i = posi; i <= posi+len-1; i++){ temp[i-posi] = begin[i]; } temp[len] = '\0'; return *(new string(temp)); } }; string& append(char ch){ char * temp = new char[length+2]; for(int i = 0;i < length;i++){ temp[i] = begin[i]; }; temp[length] = ch; temp[length + 1] = '\0'; return *(new string(temp)); }; string& insert(int posi,const string &str){ int templ = str.length + length; char * temp = new char[templ+1]; bool isAhead = false; for (int i = 0; i < templ; i++) { if(i == posi){ temp[posi] = begin[posi]; for(int j = posi+1;j <= posi + str.length;j++){ temp[j] = (str.begin[j-posi-1]); } i += posi+str.length; isAhead = !isAhead; }else{ if(isAhead){ temp[i] = begin[(i-(str.length))]; }else{ temp[i] = begin[i]; } } } temp[templ] = '\0'; return *(new string(temp)); }; string& erase(int posi,int len){ bool isAhead = false; char * temp = new char[length-len+1]; for(int i = 0;i < length;i++){ if(i == posi){ temp[posi] = begin[posi]; i += len; isAhead = !isAhead; }else{ if(isAhead){ temp[i-len] = begin[i]; }else{ temp[i] = begin[i]; } } } return *(new string(temp)); };}; 测试结果： 12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152535455#include <iostream>#include "string.h"using std::endl;using std::cout;int main(){ string str1 = "hello"; string str2; string str3(str1); string str4("huck you"); string str5(10,'j'); string str6("asdfghjklzxcvbnm"); string str7("0123456789"); cout << str1 << " 运算符重载" << endl; cout << (str1 == str2 ?"the same" : "different") << endl; cout << (str1 == str1 ?"the same" : "different") << endl; cout << (str1 != str2 ?"the same" : "different") << endl; cout << (str1 != str1 ?"the same" : "different") << endl; cout << str1[2] << endl; cout << str6.substr(2,5) << endl; cout << (str1.append('l')) << endl; cout << (str2.isEmpty()? "yes,its empty":"it has something in it") << endl; cout << (str6.insert(4,str1)) << endl; cout << (str7.erase(2,5)) << endl; cout << str3 << endl; cout << str4 << endl; cout << str5 << endl; cout << str5.getlength() << endl; cout << str5.c_str() << " 返回c类型的string" << endl;}output:hello 运算符重载differentthe samethe samedifferentldfghjhellolyes,its emptyasdfghello01289hellohuck youjjjjjjjjjj10jjjjjjjjjj 返回c类型的stringdeconstructed successfullydeconstructed successfullydeconstructed successfullydeconstructed successfullydeconstructed successfullydeconstructed successfullydeconstructed successfully

posts.title: 《津巴多普通心理学》-2，感觉和perception
posts.text: definition:sensation: the process by which the receptor creates a pattern of neural messages that present the stimulus in the brainperception: a mental process that elaborates and assigns meaning to the incoming sensory patterns their relation: perception reinterpret the sensation giving sensation meanings Core concept 2.1:the brain senses the world inderitly by receving the neural messages converted from stimulation by sense organs the info flow:stimulation -> transduction -> sensation -> perception the factors determining whether and how the stimulation is converted into senation: transductions sensory adaptation thresholds(value) fuzzy boundary weber’s laws(the same change makes less difference when the intensity is higher) the signal detection thery: sensation depends on the charactristic of the stimulus background stimulation detectortips: It may seem incredible that basic sensations, such as the redness and flavor of our tomato—or the colors Jonathan could see before his accident—are entirely creations of the sense organs and brain. The whole process (transduction) seems so immediate and direct that it fools us into assuming that the sensation of redness is characteristic of a tomato or the sensation of cold is a characteristic of ice cream. But they are not! We should consider our senses to be changedetectors. But because they accommodate to unchangingstimulation, we become less and less aware of constantstimulation. core concept 3.2The senses all operate in much the same way, but each extracts different information and sends it to its own specialized processing region in the brain. the manifestation of the sensory difference between humans:to the extent that the sense receptors exhibit somevariation from one person to another, so does our sensory experience of the world. This variability is not so bizarre as to make one person’s sensation of sweet the same as another person’s sensation of sour. Rather, the variations observed involve simply the intensity of taste sensations, such as the bitter detected by supertasters pain:pain can arise from intense stimulation of various kinds, such as a very loud sound, extremely bright light, or heavy pressure. however, the pain is not merely the result of the intensive stimulation but is from the moods and expectations.*nociceptors:*a kind of cell which exist throughout our body recevie the stimulations , convert it into neural signal and send it to central nervous system. core concept3.3:Perception brings meaning to sensation, so perception produces an interpretation of the world, not a perfect representation of it. **percept:**is not just a sensation but also the related meaning perceptual processing:the primary visual cortex,at the back of the brain,splits visual infomation into two interconnected streams.one tothe what pathway: mainly determine what the the objects are.another to*the where pathway:*mainlt dtermine the objects location.curious phenomenon about this two pathway:a curious phenomenon known as blindsight, a condition that occurs in some people with damage to the what pathway—damage that makes them visually unaware of objects around them. Yet if the where pathway is in- tact, blindsight patients may be able to step over objects in their path or reach out and touch objects that they claim not to see. after flowing throught the previous two pathway.more specialied processing will be conducted and specific features like length slant color boundary etc will be extracted out. processing patterns: top-down processing and bottom-up processing perceptual ambiguity and distortion:we can only feel illusion explaination on how we form our perception: balabalabal

posts.title: 《Psychology-core concepts》-3，学习与人的培养
posts.text: definition:learning: a process through which experience cause a lasting change in behavior or metal process forms of learning: habituation: learn not to respond to the not important stimulation. this exsit in all animals with nervous systems helping to focus on important stimulation. mere-exposure effects: people tend to have a preference for things merely because they had exposed more. behavioral learning: a connection between two stimuli.such as we assocciate a ccertain scent with a particular per who wears fragrance. classical conditioning: is a basic form of learning in which a stimulus that produces an innate reflex becomes associated with a previously neutral stimulus, which then acquires the power to elicit essentially the same response.就是这玩意，产生了-体验-这种玄而又玄的东西。 tips: some fears learned under highly stressful conditions can persist for years.During World War II, the Navy used a gong sounding at the rate of 100 rings a minute as a call to battle stations. For combat personnel aboard ship, this sound became strongly associated with danger—a CS for emotional arousal. The persistent effect of this association was shown in a study conducted 15 years after the war. classical conditioning:(passive and involuntary)CS will turn into UCS,maybe.conditional response can be extincted,however Pavlov’s dogs: Some time after undergoing extinction training, they would salivate again when they heard the tone. this is called spontaneous recovery which occurs when the CR reappers after extinction and after a peroid withoout exposure to the CS(conditional stimulus).**generalization learning:**we will have condition response to the things similar to the CS(conditional stimulus),For example, if you have developed a fear of spiders,you probably respond the same way to spiders of all size and shapes…**discrimination learning:**almost is the opposite to the generalization learning.you may salivate at the sound of lunch bell but may not salivate at the sound of the door bell. Real-World Applications of Classical Conditioning： dissuade wild coyotes from attacking sheep: They did so by wrapping toxic lamb burgers in sheepskins and stashing them on sheep ranches: When roaming coyotes found and ate these meaty morsels, they became sick and—as predicted—developed a distaste for lamb meat. The result was a whopping 30 to 50 percent reduction in sheep attacks! So powerful was this aversion that, when captured and placed in a cage with a sheep, the coyotes would not get close to it. Some even vomited at the sight of a sheep (Garcia, 1990). make doing something more comfortable: connect the things you want to optimize with pleasing things.For example ,keep your sleeping area quiet and peaceful so that when you can gain a great willing to sleep and then have a good night. tips: Conditioning involves both nature and nurture.That is, conditioning depends not only on the learned relationship among stimuli and responses but also on the way an organism is genetically attuned to certain stimuli in its environment operant conditioning:(voluntary, sometimes even suffering and operant)**core concept 4.2:**In operant conditioning, the consequences of behavior, such as rewards and punishments, influence the probability that the behavior will occur again.concequence:the most powerful influences on behavior are its consequences*law of effect(趋利避害):*The idea was that an animal’s behavior leads to pleasant or unpleasant results that influence whether the animal will try those behaviors again. *continuous reinforcement:*rewarding every correct response and ignoring the incorrect ones provide quick and clear feedback about which responses are desired. it is useful for shaping the complex new behavior.*intermittent reinforcement:*the rewarding of some, but not all, correct responses.intermittent reinforcement is the most efficient way to maintain behaviors that have already been learned A big advantage of intermittent reinforcement is its resistance to extinction.because we cant know whether the reward is going to appear. schdules of reinforcement: ratio schedule:number of response fixed ratio:a certain amount of pay for a certain amount of production.usually used in industry variable ratio interval schedule:time interval of response fixed interval: variable interval: sort: primary reinforcer: water reinforce thirsty one ,food reinforce hunary one,sex reinforce horny one. secondary reinforcer(or conditioned reinforcer): money cant be ate and be drink,it merely something created by human.however , it can also reinforce us,because it means more primary reinforcer we can have. punishment:positive punishment: application of an aversive stimulusnagative punishment: removal of a reinforcer When you do decide to use punishment, it should meet the following conditions: Punishment should be swift Punishment should be consistent Punishment should be limited in duration and intensity Punishment should clearly target the behavior and be a logical consequence of the behavior rather than an attack on character of the person (humiliation, sarcasm, or verbal abuse) or physical pain. Punishment should be limited to the situation in which the response occurred. Punishment should not give mixed messages to the punished person The most effective punishment is usually negative punishment checklist for modify operant behavior:(if you want to change someone’s behavior) positive reinforcement negative reinforcement extinction punishment comparison: classical conditioning operant conditioning Behavior is controlled by stimuli that precede the response (by the CS and UCS). Behavior is controlled by consequences (rewards, punishments, and the like) that follow the response. No reward or punishment is involved (although pleasant and aversive stimuli may be used). Often involves reward (reinforcement) or punishment. Through conditioning, a new stimulus (the CS) comes to produce “old” (reflexive) behavior. Through conditioning, a new stimulus (a reinforcer) produces new behavior. Extinction is produced by withholding the UCS. Extinction is produced by withholding reinforcement. Learner is passive (responds reflexively): Responses are involuntary. That is, behavior is elicited by stimulation. Learner is active (operant behavior): Responses are voluntary. That is, behavior is emitted by the organism.

posts.title: 上个学期写的个人博客的前端代码（其实只有前端）
posts.text: 原理利用github page来得到markdown文件，在前端解析得到博客（marked.js）的内容。 主页有头像和相关的网站，个人简介，代码水平，侧边按钮有博客，主页等按钮。 能正常使用，更新只需在特定文件夹下放入markdown文件，然后提交到github远程仓库 github

posts.title: 关于for，while，do..while的一些想法
posts.text: 1.背景本人长期受for等循环语句的困扰，每次在写循环时都要在脑子里面推演比较长的一段时间。为了加快思考速度，我总结了一些想法，有可能是各位有更好的方法 2.想法2.1 循环的通用模型循环可以看作是对状态（S，可以代表此时的内存中的变量的值）的判断和转换for循环：判断（$ S_0 $） $ \rightarrow $ 运行（可能伴随着状态变化$ S_0 \rightarrow S_1 $） $ \rightarrow $ 判断（$ S_1 $） $ \rightarrow \dots$while循环：判断（$ S_0 $） $ \rightarrow $ 运行（可能伴随着状态变化$ S_0 \rightarrow S_1 $） $ \rightarrow $ 判断（$ S_1 $） $ \rightarrow \dots$do…while循环： 运行（可能伴随着状态变化$ S_0 \rightarrow S_1 $） $ \rightarrow $ 判断 $ \rightarrow $ 运行（可能伴随着状态变化$ S_1 \rightarrow S_2 $） $ \rightarrow $ 判断 $ \rightarrow \dots$ 结论：$ （S_{end} 为跳出循环的条件 ）$分析循环时，可以列出$ S_0,S_1,S_2,S_3\dots S_{end} $一系列的状态，而由于无论那个循环都是的运行一定接着判断且跳出循环时一定是判断时，所以，进入循环运行内部的状态只有 $ S_0,S_1,S_2,S_3\dots S_{end-1} $ 使用：示例一：将一个数组复制到另一端内存上(伪代码) 可以将old[i]指向第一个元素时的i = 0为$ S_0 $而目标状态为old[i]指向old的最后一个元素的i = old.size()-1为需要进入循环中运行的状态$ S_{end-1} $所以判断跳出的条件为$ S_{end} $对应i = old.size() 1234567891011121314151617181920{vector old;vector to;int i = 0;for(int i = 0;i < old.size();){ to[i] = old[i];}while(i < old.size()){ to[i] = old[i]; i++;}i = 0;do{ to[i] = old[i]; i++;}while(i < old.size())} 示例二: 复杂一些，两个数（当然也可以一个）。一个从数组开头，向后移动，一个在数组最后向前移动,让数组倒置，当两者相遇并交换后停止$ S_{0} $ head = 0; bottom = vector.size() - 1;$ S_{end-1} $ 为 bottom - head = ((vector.size() % 2)? 0:1)$ S_{end} $ 为 bottom - head = ((vector.size() % 2)? 0:1) + 2 123456789101112{ vector vector; Type elem; int head = 0, bottom = vector.size()-1; for(;bottom - head = ((vector.size() % 2)? 0:1) + 2;) { elem = vector[head]; vector[head] = vector[bottom]; vector[bottom] = elem; head++;bottom++; }} 这个方法可能有点傻，但是还是很清晰的，至少不会把人绕晕。但是有一定的局限，就是状态之间的转换不一定能很明白的被人理解，比如$ S_{0} \rightarrow S_{1} \rightarrow S_{2} \rightarrow S_{1} \rightarrow S_{2} \rightarrow \dots$ 并且状态本身可能很复杂。

posts.title: 函数指针，函数指针数组，返回函数指针数组的函数......
posts.text: 03.07 没跑通03.08 终于通了12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243#include <iostream>#include <string>using namespace std;typedef double* (*funcs[])(double&,double&,double&);typedef double* (*funcss)(double&,double&,double&);enum func{plus,substruct,division,multiply};template <class T>double *pluss(T &a,T &b,double &origin){ origin = a+b; return &origin;}template <class T>double *substructs(T &a,T &b,double &origin){ origin = a-b; return &origin;}template <class T>double *divisions(T &a,T &b,double &origin){ origin = a/b; return &origin;}template <class T>double *multiplys(T &a,T &b,double &origin){ origin = a*b; return &origin;}funcs *getArray(){ void * arrays; arrays = (funcs *)malloc(sizeof(int)); funcs array = {pluss<double>,substructs<double>,divisions<double>,multiplys<double>}; (void *)arrays; arrays = (void *)&array; return (funcs *)arrays;}int main(){ char *aaa = "hello"; cout << aaa; funcs *ooo = getArray(); double aa = 0.0; double bb = 2.2; double cc = 2.0; cout << *((*ooo)[func::plus])(bb,cc,aa) << endl;}

posts.title: 排序算法-冒泡，选择，插入，希尔-个人实现c++
posts.text: 123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566676869707172737475767778798081828384858687888990919293949596#include <iostream>#include "string.h"using std::endl;using std::cout;template <typename T>void bubble_sort(T* array,int scale){ T temp; for(int i=scale-2;i!=-1;i--){ int jend = i+1; for(int j=0;j != jend;j++){ if(array[j] > array[i+1]){ temp = array[j]; array[j] = array[i+1]; array[i+1] = temp; } } }}template <typename T>void select_sort(T* array,int scale){ int i_end = scale-1; T temp; int record = 0; for(int i = 0;i != i_end;i++) { record = i; for(int j=i;j != scale;j++) { if(array[j] < array[record]) { record = j; } } temp = array[record]; array[record] = array[i]; array[i] = temp; }}template <typename T>void insert_sort(T* array,int scale){ T temp; for(int i = 1;i != scale;i++) { temp = array[i]; for (int j = i-1; j != -1; j--) { if(temp >= array[j]) { array[j+1] = temp; break; }else{ array[j+1] = array[j]; array[j] = temp; //比较耗时长,比较有一个读a和b值得过程，然后比较要建立一个新的区间存储比较结果，而复制只是读取b的值，然后存储到a的位置，根本不需要读取a原来的值。 continue; //if(j = 0){array(1) = array[0]; array[0] = temp} } //其实也不一定，比如非基本数据结构赋值可能就慢得多。 } }}template<typename T>void shell_sort(T* array,int scale){for (int i = (scale)/2; i != 0; i/=2){ for (int j = 0; j != i; j++) { T temp; for (int k = 1;((k+1)*i+j) < scale+1;k++) { temp = array[j+k*i]; for(int l = k-1; l != -1 ;l--) { if(temp >= array[j+l*i]) { array[j+(l+1)*i] = temp; break; }else { array[j+(l+1)*i] = array[j+(l)*i]; array[j+l*i] = temp; continue; } } } }}}int main(){int ok[] = {9,7,4,6,5,2,5,7,5,3,67,78,1,2};shell_sort<int>(ok,14);for(int i = 0; i != 14;i++){ cout << ok[i] << " ";}}

posts.title: 排序算法-归并-个人实现c++
posts.text: 12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152535455template<typename T>void merge_sort(T* array, int scale){ if(scale == 1) return; else if(scale == 2) { if (array[0] > array[1]) { T temp = array[0]; array[1] = array[0]; array[0] = temp; return; }else { return; } } else { int middle = scale>>1; T* second = array + middle; merge_sort<T>(array,middle); merge_sort<T>(second,scale-middle); T* container = new T[scale]; while (array != second || second != array + scale) { if (*array > *second) { *container = *second; container++; second++; }else { *container = *array; container++; second++; } } for (T* i = (array == second)? second: array; i != (array == second)?array+scale:second; i++) { *container = *i; } delete [] container; }}int main(){ int scale =10; int ok[] = {9,18,75,6,58,4,73,2,81,0}; shell_sort<int>(ok, scale); for (int i = 0; i != scale; i++) { cout << ok[i] << " "; }}

posts.text: setTimeout(function(){ console.log('ok') var height = document.getElementById("player").offsetWidth; document.getElementById("player").style = "width:" + height + "px;" + " height:" + (3/4)*height + "px"; console.log("load it") },5000)
